assets.description: Side-by-side PowerShell (left, primary) and Bash (right) panel showing the 6 exchanges: confirm MY_NAME is empty, set it ($env:MY_NAME = "Elvis" on PowerShell, export MY_NAME="Elvis" on Bash), read it back, use it in a greeting, list the single env var (Get-ChildItem Env:MY_NAME / printenv MY_NAME), then show a fresh terminal window returning a blank value

PNG render of the same 6-exchange side-by-side panel. PowerShell is the primary (left) shell and uses $env:MY_NAME end-to-end. The Bash column uses export and printenv to underline the shell-variable vs environment-variable distinction. The final exchange in both columns is drawn in a fresh window so the reader sees the capstone payoff (the whiteboard is wiped) at a glance
Typewriter animation of the 6-exchange PowerShell-primary walkthrough, rendered with a 48-colour adaptive palette and typing_step=2 to stay well under the 2 MB gate

PowerShell-first screen-reader transcript of the 6-exchange session with the explicit fresh-window break between exchanges 5 and 6, plus a cross-shell takeaway that explains the shell-variable vs environment-variable distinction and names the .bashrc / $PROFILE persistence path covered in Going further
